items.title: Decision automation
items.description: Credit scoring, fraud detection, and inventory forecasting use AI to rank risk or demand faster than manual review—typically narrow ML models with business rules layered on top.

items.title: Perception systems
items.description: Speech recognition, OCR, and object detection translate raw sensor data into structured signals other software can act on—core AI perception tasks in products and robotics.

items.title: Planning and optimization
items.description: Route planners, factory schedulers, and game engines search large decision spaces—classical AI complements learning-based approaches in logistics and operations.

items.title: Human–computer interaction
items.description: Chatbots, copilots, and recommendation surfaces embed AI so users state goals in natural language or implicit behavior instead of writing bespoke queries.
